sections.paragraph:
εὐπρεπής, ές gen. οῦς (s. prec. entry and πρέπω; Aeschyl., Hdt.+; Diod S 1, 45, 4 Vogel v.l. [the text has ναοῖς ἐκπρεπέσι]; Epict. 1, 2, 18 al.; ins; POxy 1380, 130 [II a.d.]; LXX; TestAbr A; JosAs; Philo; Jos., Ant. 6, 160; Ath. 33, 2). Comp. εὐπρεπέστερος (Aeschyl. et al.; Wsd 7:29) Dg 2:2; Hs 5, 2, 4. Superl. εὐπρεπέστατος (Hdt. et al.; TestAbr A 2 p. 78, 18 [Stone p. 4]; Jos., Ant. 1, 200) Hs 2:1; 9, 1, 10; 9, 10, 3.

① pert. to having an attractive appearance, looking well of a vineyard carefully dug and weeded εὐπρεπέστερος ἔσται it will look better Hs 5, 2, 4. Of a white mountain εὐπρεπέστατον ἦν ἐν ἑαυτῷ τὸ ὄρος the mountain by itself (without any trees) was most beautiful 9, 1, 10. Of a place that has been cleaned very attractive 9, 10, 3. Of a building magnificent 9, 9, 7. οὐδὲν εὐπρεπέστερον w. gen. no better to look at than Dg 2:2.

② pert. to being compatible or harmonious, suited τινί to someth. of the elm and the vine εὐπρεπέσταταί εἰσιν ἀλλήλαις they are very well suited to each other Hs 2:1.

gen. gen. = genitive

prec. prec. = preceding

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Comp Comp date uncertain—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

Hs Hs = Similitudes

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

someth. someth. = something

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 410
